text.title: Ollama Setup, Model Selection, and API Usage
text.summary: Installation, RAM-based model selection, health check pattern, and OpenAI-compatible endpoint for local/cloud switching.

example.code:
# Install (Linux) curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
# Start server (keep as systemd service in production) ollama serve
# Pull a model (blocking — handle in agent as a blocking operation) ollama pull llama3.1:8b
# List available models ollama list

rule.statement: Keep Ollama running as a systemd service on Linux; check server availability via /api/tags at agent startup and fail fast with a clear error if Ollama is not running.
rule.rationale: Agents that assume Ollama is running will produce cryptic connection errors. A health check at startup with a descriptive error message saves debug time.

rule.statement: Use the OpenAI-compatible /v1 endpoint for all agent code — it enables local/cloud switching by changing only base_url and api_key, with zero code changes.
rule.rationale: Maintaining two code paths (Ollama API + OpenAI API) doubles testing surface. The /v1 compatibility layer makes Ollama a drop-in replacement for development.

list.item: llama3.1:8b (4.7GB) — 8GB RAM, general purpose, supports tool use

rule.statement: Use quantized variants (Q4_K_M, Q5_K_M) — they cut VRAM by 50–60% with 2–5% quality loss; log eval_count and total_duration to detect GPU fallback to CPU (10–50x slower).
rule.rationale: Unquantized models that exceed VRAM fall back to CPU inference, which is 10–50x slower. eval_count/total_duration expose this without needing nvidia-smi in the agent.
